events.title: Geertrui café with guest speaker
events.description:
Tuesday 19 May at 7.30 p.m. in Geertruihof. Guest speaker: Kris Gelaude.

Kris writes poetic and liturgical texts, columns and articles; she has published several books and gives talks on religious and existential themes.

For this Geertrui café she spoke on believing today: “How can we, in today’s world with so much bad news, find new paths together and create places of hope?”

About 55 people attended; Kris held us for an hour with inspiring examples. Afterwards there was a long chat over a soft drink.

events.title: Thank you: donations for care farm Rimpeling
events.description: Donations for the Rimpeling care farm of Greet Herkes and Katja in Baal now total €2,585; further donations may bring the total to €2,750. A very warm thank you!

events.title: Blessing of twelve new carillon bells
events.description: 11 a.m. during our closing and thanksgiving celebration at St Gertrude’s Church: Dean Patrick Maervoet solemnly blesses our twelve new carillon bells. Opportunity to view the bells up close. After the service: drinks at the back of the church.

events.title: Closing celebration — auberge espagnole
events.description:
After the 11 a.m. service we close the pastoral year with an auberge espagnole (potluck) in Geertruihof — bring dishes from 10 a.m. (stored in the fridge). Sign up: geertruifeesten@gmail.com or +32 474 72 83 60.

After the meal: Geertrui meeting for those who wish; taken forward to the team day on Saturday 27 June.

events.title: No Mass in our church (holiday schedule)
events.description: We switch to the holiday schedule: a service in our church every two weeks; on the other Sundays we join the 10 a.m. celebration at St Peter’s Church.

events.title: Solemn ringing-in of the renewed St Gertrude carillon
events.description:
11 a.m. solemn ringing-in of our renewed St Gertrude carillon (twelve bells, new keyboard) — Eucharistic celebration led by Father Arnold Castro.

12–12.30 p.m.: first official sounds by titular carillonneur Marc Van Eyck. Then addresses, lecture, carillon brochure presentation and reception; guest carillonneurs afterwards.

Listen from the abbey park, Geertruihof corridor and Klein Begijnhof. RSVP reception: geertruifeesten@gmail.com or +32 474 72 83 60.

events.title: Church temporarily closed — worship with the Assumptionists
events.description:
If you read the texts about the restoration on this website, you will see that a great deal of work has already been carried out on the building, and that much more is planned in the coming years.

From October 2026 stability work will be carried out on several pillars in the church and everything will be prepared for installing a heating system. For that reason the church will unfortunately have to be closed for public worship for six months. During that time our faith community is kindly hosted by the Assumptionist fathers, close to our church in Halve Straat. Throughout that period we can gather for Sunday services together in the monastery chapel.
